exemplar.title: Modelling The Palm Jumeirah - HL Maths Exploration

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling The Palm Jumeirah - HL Maths Exploration

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Logical progression from introduction through data, modelling, calculations and conclusion
Clear use of headings and labelled figures for easy navigation
Relevant content with mostly concise exposition
weaknesses: Long quotations of software output interrupt narrative flow
Minor formatting glitches (duplicate captions, mis-aligned tables)
Occasional unnecessary repetition in contextual sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 3: it is coherent with clear logical development, well organized into conventional sections, and mostly concise. Minor lapses in flow and formatting prevent it reaching band 4, but its overall presentation justifies a score of 3.

strengths: Consistent and correct use of notation for integrals, logarithms and ellipses
Effective deployment of multiple representations (tables, diagrams, overlaid graphs)
Generally clear explanations with symbols defined in context
weaknesses: Inconsistent variable naming (mixing âaâ/âbâ and numeric values)
Missing axis labels and scale markings on some figures
Occasional lapses in defining subscripts and derived factors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates mostly appropriate mathematical language and notation, effective use of multiple representations, and clear communication overall. Inconsistencies in notation and missing labels prevent band 4 status, supporting a final score of 3.

strengths: Personal choice of a familiar landmark drives independent decision-making
Clear narrative voice tying model choices to personal experience
Use of non-syllabus elements (ellipses, geometric decomposition) shows initiative
weaknesses: Modelling relies heavily on standard regression tools without bespoke algorithm development
The personal context, while authentic, is not fully integrated into every mathematical decision
Opportunity for deeper creative exploration (e.g. comparative models) remains
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and creativity consistent with band 2. It does not reach the outstanding cohesion or originality required for band 3, so a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model accuracy via percentage error comparison
Some critique of limitations (un-modelled tips, approximation choices)
Clear connection between reflection and mathematical understanding
weaknesses: Critique remains surface-level without exploring alternatives or sensitivity analyses
Limited depth in justifying method choices (e.g. trapezium vs rectangle)
Opportunities to reflect on cumulative rounding and transformation errors are not exploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflections are meaningful and linked to understanding, matching band 2 descriptors. They lack the depth and critical rigour for band 3, warranting a final score of 2.

strengths: Application of HL-level mathematics (regression, conic equations, integral calculus)
Correct setup of area-between-curves integrals and geometric decompositions
Use of mapping tools for primary data collection shows methodological independence
weaknesses: Several calculation errors (mis-copied axis values, sign reversals)
Reliance on technology without full analytical justification for key parameters
Some domain and variable assumptions are not explicitly addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the exploration uses relevant HL mathematics effectively, accuracy and conceptual understanding show inconsistencies. With two strands at band 3 and one at band 4, the overall performance aligns with the band 3 descriptor for use of mathematics, so a score of 3 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally relevant landmark, collecting primary coordinate data and deciding which functions to test show significant independent decisions beyond textbook examples.  However, much of the modelling relies on standard Desmos regression and textbook integration, so the evidence does not reach the âoutstandingâ threshold.

The student shows good conceptual understanding of regression, ellipse geometry and area by integration, explaining each step intelligibly.  However, occasional misconceptions (e.g., treating the ellipse equation rearrangement without discussing domain limits) and reliance on technology without full justification prevent the work from demonstrating âgood understanding throughoutâ, so the strand is credited with 3 marks for âgood understanding in partsâ.

Most calculations (ellipse parameters, integrals evaluated with technology, area summations) are correct and lead to sensible magnitudes.  Nevertheless, several inaccuracies exist: mis-copied semi-axis values, a rectangle area computed with 540 m rather than 500 m, and sign issues that required absolute values.  This places accuracy at âmostly correctâ rather than âcorrect with minor errorsâ.

Most of the writing is relevant to modelling and area estimation; however lengthy screen-shots of regression output and repeated restatement of the same numerical values reduce conciseness.  Only occasional off-task sentences appear, so the work is ârelevant and mostly conciseâ rather than âhighly concise throughoutâ.

The student employs correct notation for ellipses, integrals and logarithms and uses subscripts effectively.  A few inconsistencies appear (e.g. switching between âaâ/âbâ and numeric values mid-sentence, unclear variable renaming), hence language is âmostly appropriateâ rather than faultless.

Explanations are generally clear, and symbols are used consistently within single sections.  Occasional notation slips (mixing x_a and xc, inconsistent decimal places) introduce minor inconsistencies, placing the work in the âclear and mostly consistentâ band.

The exploration applies a wide range of relevant AI HL mathematics: logarithmic and exponential regressions, circle and ellipse equations, integral calculus for area, geometric decomposition and unit conversions.  The mathematics is clearly at the HL course level and used effectively to address the aim, though not pushed to fully sophisticated or rigorous proof, fitting the 4-mark band.

Tables of regression results, numerous labelled diagrams, graphs superimposed on the map and algebraic expressions are all used to convey ideas.  These representations complement one another effectively, but they are not refined to a sophisticated level (e.g. axes sometimes omitted, scale unclear), so the strand fits âeffective use of multiple representationsâ.

Reflection occurs when comparing the calculated area with Britannicaâs figure, computing percentage error and discussing implications for housing capacity.  These reflections are meaningful but not deeply critical; they are confined to a few paragraphs without iterative refinement, matching the descriptor for âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ.

Applying ellipses (outside the syllabus) and combining geometric decomposition with calculus shows creativity and initiative.  Still, the methods remain mostly standard and the exploration refrains from developing original algorithms or comparative models, indicating âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ creativity.

The report moves logically from an introduction and aim, through data collection, modelling of each component, area calculations and a conclusion.  Paragraph links are generally clear and each new section builds on what has already been established.  Minor lapses in flow occur where very long quotations of Desmos output interrupt the narrative, but overall the development is coherent and understandable, warranting the âcoherent with clear logical developmentâ level.

The student analyses sources of discrepancy and acknowledges modelling limitations (e.g. un-modelled branch tips, rectangle approximation).  The critique is present but remains surface-levelâno alternative methods or sensitivity analysis is carried outâso the strand sits in the âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ band.

Reflections are linked to the studentâs mathematical understanding (percentage error, impact of domain restrictions) and to real-world housing considerations, showing a clear if not profound connection to learning.  This aligns with the âclear connection to understandingâ level.

The exploration is laid out with conventional academic structure (title, introduction, data, modelling, calculation, conclusion, bibliography).  Headings are used consistently and figures are labelled, allowing the reader to locate information easily.  A few formatting glitches (duplicate captions, mis-aligned tables) prevent it from feeling totally âseamlessâ, so it fits the description of âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ.

The studentâs narrative frequently references personal experience of living in Dubai and clearly drives the direction of the exploration.  This personal voice is evident but not deeply woven into every mathematical decision, fitting the âclear personal approach that drives the explorationâ level.

annotations.comment: The personal context provided is authentic and demonstrates clear personal engagement with the topic.
The title is concise and clearly indicates the focus of the exploration, engaging the reader from the outset.
Consider adding a brief subtitle or contextual phrase to frame the aim early, guiding the reader to the investigationâs objectives.
Axis labels and scale markings on the rotated image would improve clarity and mathematical communication in Figure 3.
Reflect on potential distortion when rotating and scaling the map image, as this could introduce systematic error.
Figure 3 effectively shows plotted points; consider including a reference legend or table for quick point lookup.
Rounding to three significant figures is consistent; consider discussing the potential cumulative impact of rounding errors.
The final ellipse equation is clearly stated and ready for validation, showing good use of mathematics.
Include a quantitative fit measure (e.g. residual plot) alongside the visual overlay to support the claim of a good fit.
Transformations to derive the inner ellipse are applied correctly, demonstrating sound mathematical understanding.
Discuss the criteria used to choose exponential versus logarithmic fits for the branches to deepen critical reflection.
Calculating the theoretical maximum area with Ïab is a robust method demonstrating upperâbound reasoning.
The narrative could be more concise by trimming general tourism context; focus directly on how this motivates the mathematical modelling.
The aim statement is clear but would benefit from specifying the variables to be modelled (e.g. x- and y-coordinates) for mathematical precision.
Using a reputable mapping tool for primary data collection demonstrates good methodological independence.
Explicitly stating the symmetry assumption and its implications would strengthen critical reflection on model validity.
The general ellipse equation is correctly stated, demonstrating solid mathematical communication.
Justify the choice of ellipse over other conic sections for the crescent; discuss why circle or parabolic fits were less suitable.
Swapping a and b axes is correct, though variable naming could be more consistent to avoid reader confusion.
Calculation of semi-major axis and centre coordinate is methodical and clearly presented.
Screenshots of regression outputs provide transparency but interrupt narrative flow; summarise key parameters in a table instead.
Axis labels are missing on regression graph outputs; include them for clarity and consistency.
Summarising the final branch equations with domain restrictions in Table 1 shows excellent organization and coherence.
Ensure each equationâs variables are defined in the textâfor instance, clarify what each subscript denotes.
Justify the choice of circles for some branches and discuss their limitations, enhancing critical reflection.
Using vertical reflections to model symmetry is efficient and shows solid grasp of function transformations.
Quantify the area of un-modelled tip regions to reflect on their impact on the final area estimate.
Derivation of y = Â±â... for ellipse integration is clear and mathematically rigorous.
Clarify how the factor (255/212)^2 was derived in the rearrangement to strengthen mathematical communication.
The structure of the areaâbetweenâcurves integrals is exemplary and shows strong use of mathematics.
Explain the origin of the 1452.84 m value used in the rectangle calculation to ensure transparency in methodology.
Negative branch area indicates reversed integrand order; ensure correct function order to avoid sign errors.
Referencing detailed branch areas in the appendix is helpful but summarise key results in the main body for coherence.
Combining calculus and geometric shapes to estimate the trunk and entrance shows creativity and initiative.
Discuss why a trapezium was chosen over a simpler average-height rectangle to enhance reflective critique.
Reflect on the assumptions in population density and household size; consider sensitivity to different densities.
The percentage error calculation demonstrates meaningful reflection on model accuracy.
Clarify which sections Britannicaâs 1,380 acres refers to, reducing ambiguity in error interpretation.
Consider linking development proposals back to specific model outcomes to reinforce mathematical relevance.
Bibliography entries are complete but inconsistent in formatting; apply a uniform citation style.
